name: Kailash Satyarthi

bio: An engineer who walked away from the drawing board to walk into bonded-labour quarries, he founded Bachpan Bachao Andolan (1980), GoodWeave (1994), the Global March Against Child Labour (1998) and, most recently, the Satyarthi Movement for Global Compassion (2024). bio: Hon'ble Shri Ramdas Bandu Athawale — born 25 December 1959 in Agalgaon, Sangli — is Dalit Maharashtra's most visible political couplet-maker. Founder-President of the Republican Party of India (Athawale) since 1999, Rajya Sabha MP since 2014 (re-elected unopposed 2020), and continuously Union Minister of State for Social Justice and Empowerment since 2016 through Modi 3. 0, he is Marathi poet, Ambedkarite, Buddhist and film actor rolled into one. His rhyming Hindi verses ("Modi hai toh mumkin hai"; "Go Corona Go") sit beside a serious record on reservations and Scheduled-Caste welfare — a politician who legislates in prose and campaigns in rhyme. डू

name: Bhupesh Kumar
designation: General Secretary, Aryaman Foundation (Regd. ), Ludhiana
address: 14-D, Kitchlu Nagar, Ludhiana 141001
bio: About the Foundation: Non-profit established in memory of Mr. Aryaman Verma — Ludhiana-born Sat Paul Mittal School alumnus (Class X 94. 8%, Class XII 82%), India Book of Records "youngest technocrat" for the Shudhvayu air purifier, country's youngest drone developer, ISRO YUVIKA Young Scientist Programme selectee, 1st Prize Project Competition on Space Technology at Lovely Professional University, 1st Runner-up at South Asia's largest robotics competition (Moscow). Aryaman went on to read Computer Science at Penn State University before cancer claimed his life. The Foundation runs four flagship initiatives — SMARTH (cancer patient support), WHIZAARU (robotics & innovation skill-building for under-served youth), HOPE (mentorship), and scholarships for girls and economically disadvantaged students
honours: Republic Day 2026 — District Administration Award On 26 January 2026, the District Administration of Ludhiana formally honoured Aryaman Foundation (Regd. ) at the Republic Day function for two strands of work — "helping families battling cancer and promoting robotics education among children". The Foundation team describes the moment as "a deeply emotional and proud moment … what began as a tribute in Aryaman's memory is growing into a movement of service and responsibility. " Co-launched the Ludhiana Young Achievers Award (7 February 2025) The Foundation jointly launched the "Ludhiana Young Achievers Award" at DCM Young Entrepreneur School. The launch was led under the able guidance of Dr. Anirudh Gupta (CEO, DCM YES) and was endorsed at-presence by Mr. Jitender Jorwal, IAS (Deputy Commissioner of Ludhiana), Mr. D. R. Bhatti, IPS (Retd. ) (former DGP), Mr. Kuldip Bawa, PCS, and Mr. Prince Johar (Deputy Mayor, Ludhiana). On 21 March 2025 the inaugural ceremony facilitated 25 young achievers, each receiving certificates and gifts of appreciation at DCM YES School. Government of India recognition — NGO Darpan (21 August 2024) The Aryaman Foundation was registered with NGO Darpan, NITI Aayog, Government of India under registration PB/2024/0442848 — making it eligible to receive grants from Central Ministries and to file Requests-for-Proposals with major corporates and CSR funds. The Foundation is also registered under the Societies Registration Act with the Punjab state government (8 August 2024). Health-system partnership recognition (5 June 2025) The Foundation team met Dr. Balbir Singh, Hon'ble Minister of Health, Government of Punjab to discuss enrolling needy families in the Ayushman Bharat health-coverage scheme for cancer treatment support — a working-level recognition of the Foundation's role in last-mile patient access. On 10 August 2025, with Dr. Balbir Singh and the Philanthropy Club, the Foundation participated in the inauguration of the new Thalassemia Ward at Civil Hospital, Ludhiana, donating toys to paediatric thalassemia patients. Notable peer-recognised initiatives (not formal awards but cited publicly): SMARTH — paediatric cancer support programmeme; supported children including Pratik (DMCH), Sehar (3 yrs, CMC), Rupa (1 yr 8 m, kidney tumour, CMC), Rohandeep (19 yrs, blood cancer), Khushdeep Singh (14 yrs, CMC), Jasveer Kaur (14 yrs, bone cancer, DMC), Navraj Kaur (3 months, CMC), Charu Mishra (oral cancer), Veena Sharma (SPS Apollo), Deepak Sharma's family. WHIZAARU — robotics & AI education programmeme; held WHIZFEST 2. 0, ran workshops at Government High School Sarabha Nagar, Government Girls School Bhawani Garh, Indus World School (Metaverse / MetaQuest demos), and inaugurated a Robotics-and-Computer Lab at Bal Bhawan on Diwali Eve (20 October 2025). HOPE — cancer-patient financial-emotional support. National Space Day Quiz (23 August 2024) — 250 students across Ludhiana, Kolkata, Delhi, Rajasthan, Jaipur, Pune, Varanasi, Nagpur, Udaipur, Meerut, Kanpur, Guwahati, Bangalore, Jalandhar, Chandigarh. Blood Donation Camp (15 July 2025) — 110 units collected in association with Lodhi Club and the Red Foundation, with Civil Hospital & DMCH support teams. Walkathon 2025 — flagged off 12 January 2025. No personal awards for Mr. Bhupesh Kumar that I could find on the Foundation's website, blog, or in public records. All institutional recognition flows to the Foundation collectively, with Mrs. Dimple Verma (President) named publicly as the lead spokesperson. The Republic Day 2026 award accepted on the Foundation's behalf is the most significant formal honour to date, and Mr. Bhupesh Kumar — as the Foundation's General Secretary — would be one of the office-bearers in whom that recognition vests

bio: A Kanpur-based educationist and entrepreneur, Dr. Chandan Agarwal founded G. D. Goenka Public School, Kanpur in 2013 as a franchise partner of the G. D. Law-trained in Moscow, economics-trained in Leningrad, she met Robert Louis-Dreyfus on a 1988 flight and married him in 1992; on his death in 2009, she took the chair of LDC — one of the world's "ABCD" commodity houses founded in 1851. Through her Akira family trust she holds 96. 2% of the Group; in 2016 she sold Olympique de Marseille, in 2020 she welcomed Abu Dhabi's ADQ as partner. Zurich-based, mother of five, she arrives at the Mumbai Conclave as a steward of continuity — wheat, rice, sugar, coffee, cotton, rice and dairy all moving quietly beneath her gaze

In November 2020 LDC announced a strategic agreement to sell an indirect 45% equity stake to ADQ (Abu Dhabi sovereign holding), alongside a long-term supply arrangement with the UAE. Her son Kyril is Chairman and majority shareholder of English Premier League club Sunderland AFC. Net worth approximately US$5 billion (December 2024, Mabumbe / TheCityCeleb); peak US$9. 5 billion in 2016 (Forbes). Fortune Most Powerful Women in Europe, Middle East & Africa list (2015 onwards). qualifications: Administrator of the State (second grade) / Sous-préfet — a senior French civil servant career grade. Served at the Ministry of the Interior from 2016 to 2025, holding the positions of Sub-Prefect of Boulogne-sur-Mer (July 2022 – late 2025), Largentière (Ardèche) and other postings

bio: Ms Leymah Roberta Gbowee is the Liberian peace activist and social worker whose white-clad sit-ins at the Monrovia fish market helped end a fourteen-year civil war. A 2011 Nobel Peace Laureate — honoured with Ellen Johnson Sirleaf and Tawakkol Karman "for their non-violent struggle for the safety of women and for women's rights to full participation in peace-building work" — she founded the Gbowee Peace Foundation Africa in Monrovia in February 2012 and now also directs CUNY's Institute on Gender, Law, and Transformative Peace. Trained trauma counsellor, memoirist of Mighty Be Our Powers, mother of six: an ordinary woman who refused to let the bullets have the last word
honours: NOBEL PEACE PRIZE 2011 (jointly with Ellen Johnson Sirleaf of Liberia and Tawakkol Karman of Yemen). Exact Nobel citation (nobelprize. org): "for their non-violent struggle for the safety of women and for women's rights to full participation in peace-building work". Nobel Lecture delivered Oslo, 10 December 2011. Other honours: Blue Ribbon for Peace (2007); John F. Kennedy Profile in Courage Award; Gruber Prize for Women's Rights; Living Legends Award for Service to Humanity; Martin & Coretta King Inaugural Peace & Justice Award (2020); Columbia SIPA Global Leadership Award (2019). Narrator and central figure of the 2008 documentary Pray the Devil Back to Hell (Best Documentary Feature, Tribeca 2008). Author, memoir Mighty Be Our Powers (2011)
qualifications: BA, Mother Patern College of Health Sciences, Monrovia (social work); MA in Conflict Transformation, Eastern Mennonite University, Virginia, USA (earned). Numerous honorary doctorates from Rhodes College, University of Alberta, Polytechnic University of Mozambique, Georgetown University (2024), and others
